title: Interactive Brokers Group, Inc. (IBKR) — Intrinsic Value & Prospect Score
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Interactive Brokers (IBKR) compiled entirely by Gemini. Economic Prospect Score: 84/100 — Strong Prospect.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: A reliable free cash flow growth rate cannot be modeled as the base year free cash flow data was not available or standard definition does not apply easily to financial firms.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: Not calculated due to the inability to perform a standard DCF analysis without a reliable base free cash flow.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: Not applicable as a terminal value cannot be reliably calculated.

thesisParagraphs: Interactive Brokers operates with a distinct advantage in the brokerage industry: relentless automation. By eschewing physical branches and extensive marketing in favor of a technology-driven platform, it achieves industry-leading margins exceeding 92%. This structure allows it to offer the lowest costs to clients while maintaining high profitability.

Due to the nature of its business as a financial services firm and lack of reported standard Free Cash Flow metrics in the queried data, a traditional DCF model is not provided here. The company's value is deeply tied to its net interest margin, trading volumes, and continued ability to attract active, sophisticated investors globally.
verdictFooter: Based on latest available data. Data via yfinance (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: Why is there no Computed IV for IBKR?
faq.answer: A standard Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model relies on projecting future free cash flows. For many financial institutions like brokerages, standard free cash flow is difficult to define or wasn't available in the data source, making a reliable intrinsic value calculation impossible using this specific method.

faq.question: What drives Interactive Brokers' profitability?
faq.answer: Profitability is driven by its incredibly low cost structure achieved through automation, coupled with revenue from trading commissions and significant net interest income generated from client cash balances and margin loans.

faq.question: What is IBKR's gross margin?
faq.answer: Interactive Brokers boasts an exceptional gross margin of approximately 92.5%, highlighting the immense operating leverage of its automated platform.

prospectVerdictDetail: Interactive Brokers presents a robust prospect profile driven by its low-cost structure, global reach, and highly automated platform. With strong revenue growth of 15.2% and exceptional gross margins exceeding 92%, its competitive momentum is formidable. The platform's stickiness among active traders and institutions creates a durable moat, supporting sustained long-term value creation.

competitiveMomentum.title: Competitive Momentum
competitiveMomentum.summary: Interactive Brokers demonstrates excellent competitive momentum, characterized by double-digit revenue growth and industry-leading margins.

factors.name: Revenue Growth vs. Peers
factors.rationale: The company is achieving a strong 15.2% revenue growth rate. This outpaces many traditional and discount brokerages, driven by account growth and trading activity.

factors.name: Market Share Trajectory
factors.rationale: IBKR continues to consistently gain market share, particularly among active traders, hedge funds, and introducing brokers globally.

factors.name: Pricing Power
factors.rationale: While competing on low costs, its exceptional 92.5% gross margin indicates immense operating leverage and the ability to maintain profitability even in competitive pricing environments.

factors.name: Product Velocity
factors.rationale: The platform consistently expands its asset class offerings and global market access. Its API and institutional tools remain highly regarded and continually updated.

moatDurability.title: Moat Durability
moatDurability.summary: The durability of IBKR's moat is built on high switching costs for its core user base and significant economies of scale.

factors.name: Switching Costs
factors.rationale: For active traders, hedge funds, and financial advisors, the operational friction of moving complex portfolios, algorithms, and integrations to another platform is substantial.

factors.name: Network Effects
factors.rationale: While not a traditional network effect, growing trading volume improves liquidity and order routing efficiency, indirectly benefiting all users.

factors.name: Regulatory & IP Position
factors.rationale: Operating across numerous global jurisdictions requires complex regulatory compliance, creating a barrier to entry for new competitors.

factors.name: Capital Intensity Advantage
factors.rationale: The highly automated, technology-first platform requires very low incremental capital to service new clients, driving massive scalability.

sentimentCatalyst.title: Sentiment & Catalysts
sentimentCatalyst.summary: Sentiment remains positive, supported by strong financial performance and continued international expansion.

factors.name: Earnings Estimate Revisions
factors.rationale: Consistent revenue growth and high margins generally support positive earnings estimate revisions, reflecting confidence in the business model.

factors.name: News & Narrative Sentiment
factors.rationale: The narrative is favorable, focusing on IBKR's status as a premier platform for sophisticated investors and its successful capture of global market share.

factors.name: Management & Capital Allocation
factors.rationale: Management has a long track record of disciplined execution, focusing on automation and technology rather than expensive marketing campaigns.

prospectKeyRisks: Significant sensitivity to global interest rates; a rapid decline in rates could compress net interest margins.
A severe and prolonged downturn in global equity markets could reduce trading volumes and margin borrowing.
Intense competition from emerging fintech platforms and established zero-commission brokerages.
prospectKeyCatalysts: Continued expansion into new international markets, particularly in emerging economies with growing middle classes.
Increasing adoption by registered investment advisors (RIAs) and institutional clients seeking a low-cost, comprehensive platform.
A higher-for-longer interest rate environment sustaining elevated net interest income.
prospectMethodology: Consensus Analysis — Economic Prospect Score averaging independent evaluations from Opus 4.6 and Gemini 3.1. Gemini scored IBKR at 82/100 and Opus at 87/100. Each factor score is the arithmetic mean of both models. Three pillars: Competitive Momentum (0-35), Moat Durability (0-35), and Sentiment & Catalysts (0-30).
